---
created_at: 2026-03-30T09:20:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
category: onboarding
---

# ProjectX — New Team Member Onboarding Guide

## 1) Purpose

This guide helps new engineers get productive quickly on **ProjectX** by covering:

- Local environment setup
- Repository structure and core workflows
- Key code conventions
- Commonly used commands

## 2) Prerequisites

### Required Accounts / Access
- GitHub access to the ProjectX repository
- Access to shared environments (staging/dev), if applicable
- Credentials for:
  - PostgreSQL instance (local or dev)
  - Redis instance (local or dev)
  - Any auth/payment sandbox secrets used by the team

### Required Tools
- **Node.js 18+**
- **PostgreSQL 14+**
- **Redis 6+**
- **Docker + Docker Compose** (recommended)

## 3) Repository Setup

### 3.1 Clone and Install
```bash
git clone https://github.com/company/projectx.git
cd projectx
npm install
```

### 3.2 Environment Variables
```bash
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your local/dev configuration
```

Common `.env` sections include:
- Database connection settings
- Redis connection settings
- Authentication provider configs (OAuth/SAML)
- Payment gateway sandbox credentials
- API rate limiting thresholds

### 3.3 Database Initialization
```bash
npm run db:migrate
npm run db:seed
```

### 3.4 Run the App
```bash
npm run dev
```

Default local URL:
- `http://localhost:3000`

## 4) Docker-Based Setup (Optional / Recommended)

If you prefer a containerized environment:

```bash
docker-compose up -d
npm install
npm run db:migrate
npm run db:seed
npm run dev
```

Tip: confirm container health before running migrations.

## 5) Key Code Conventions

### 5.1 General
- Keep changes small and reviewable (prefer multiple PRs over one large PR).
- Add or update documentation for any user-visible behavior changes.
- Follow semantic versioning expectations for API changes.

### 5.2 TypeScript / JavaScript
- Prefer **TypeScript** types over `any`.
- Avoid implicit `any` and unsafe casts.
- Keep functions small; extract helpers for complex business logic.

### 5.3 API Design
- APIs are **RESTful** and documented via **OpenAPI 3.0**.
- When adding/changing endpoints:
  - Update the OpenAPI spec in the same PR.
  - Add integration tests for API endpoints.
  - Consider rate limiting and auth requirements.

### 5.4 Testing Expectations
- **New code** should meet the quality bar:
  - ≥ **80% coverage** for new logic where measurable
  - Integration tests for API endpoints
  - E2E tests (Cypress) for critical user flows

### 5.5 Documentation Rule (Docs Before Merge)
- New features and changes are expected to have docs **before merge**.
- Feature owners write user guides/notes; API docs are generated from OpenAPI.

## 6) Commonly Used Commands

### Development
```bash
npm run dev
```

### Tests
```bash
npm test                 # Unit tests
npm run test:integration # Integration tests
npm run test:e2e         # End-to-end tests
```

### Database
```bash
npm run db:migrate
npm run db:seed
```

### Lint / Format (if configured)
```bash
npm run lint
npm run format
```

### Build / Production (if configured)
```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

## 7) Day-1 Checklist

- [ ] Clone repo + install dependencies
- [ ] `.env` configured
- [ ] DB/Redis running (local or Docker)
- [ ] Migrations + seed completed
- [ ] App starts locally
- [ ] Can run unit + integration tests
- [ ] Read: API versioning ADR + current sprint goals

## 8) Where to Ask Questions

- Engineering chat channel for daily questions
- Tag the feature owner for domain-specific areas (auth, payments, admin dashboard)
- If blocked > 30 minutes, post a short summary:
  - what you tried
  - error logs
  - what you need (access/decision/help)
